title: kubectl Discovery, Contexts, Queries, Diff, Apply, and Safe Mutation · Control-Plane Trace: K8s Apply Diff Field Management
summary: An operator has two similarly named clusters and a manifest owned by multiple field managers; a convenient imperative edit could hit the wrong authority and steal fields. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s apply diff field management increment in safe kubectl investigation and apply record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Preview and apply declarative changes while tracing field managers, conflicts, pruning scope, server-side apply, and changed status.
Choose get, describe, events, logs, JSONPath, custom columns, selectors, and raw JSON or YAML without parsing human tables as stable interfaces.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example operator is pointed at production instead of training, relies on table output, runs client dry-run, and plans an unscoped prune. Make context, namespace, server validation, field ownership, diff, and UID-safe mutation explicit. Competency probe: Preview and apply declarative changes while tracing field managers, conflicts, pruning scope, server-side apply, and changed status. A defensible solution applies K8s Apply Diff Field Management, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to safe kubectl investigation and apply record.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-apply-diff-field-management — Preview and apply declarative changes while tracing field managers, conflicts, pruning scope, server-side apply, and changed status.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-kubectl-contexts-declarative-2
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-kubectl-contexts-declarative-2
    artifact: kubectl-plan.yaml
    decision: worked k8s kubectl contexts declarative 2
    environment: rootless development cluster with an empty namespace
    fault-injection: make an admission dependency unavailable during an upgrade
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      context: training-cluster
      namespace: intake
      manager: learn-it-all
      preview: server-dry-run-and-diff
      mutation: server-side-apply
      delete: uid-precondition
    verify: kubectl config current-context && kubectl diff --server-side -f release.yaml
    failure: Reject implicit context, human-table parsing, client dry run as admission proof, unscoped prune, or field ownership theft.
    repair: Name cluster and namespace, use discovery and structured output, preview server behavior, scope ownership, and verify changed status.
